title: Biometrics and Privacy-Preservation: How Do They Evolve?
venue: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
notes: PRIMARY survey paper. Comprehensive taxonomy of privacy-preserving biometrics: Non-invertible Transformation, Direct Biometric Key Generation, Information Hiding, Protocol-based Protection. Provides theoretical foundation for ZKBIOWN approach. References ISO/IEC 24745 standard for template protection.

title: Cancelable Biometrics: A Review
notes: Alternative survey paper (older but well-cited). Good for historical context of cancelable biometrics development.

title: Random Multispace Quantization as an Analytic Mechanism for BioHashing of Biometric and Random Identity Inputs
venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
notes: ⭐ PRIMARY BioHashing citation - MATCHES YOUR CODE IMPLEMENTATION! Your experimental/utils/biohashing.ts explicitly implements this methodology: Gaussian random projection with Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization. Code comments reference "Teoh, Ngo, Goh (IEEE TPAMI 2006)". This is THE paper to cite for your methodology section.

title: BioHashing: Two Factor Authentication Featuring Fingerprint Data and Tokenised Random Number
notes: Original BioHashing paper (fingerprint-based). Cite for historical context, but Teoh 2006 is the primary methodology reference.

title: Poseidon: A New Hash Function for Zero-Knowledge Proof Systems
venue: Proceedings of the 30th USENIX Security Symposium
notes: ⭐ PRIMARY Poseidon citation. ZK-friendly hashing optimized for R1CS constraints. Achieves 0.2-0.5 constraints/bit vs Pedersen Hash (1.68 constraints/bit). Provides performance benchmarks for Groth16, Bulletproofs, PLONK, STARKs. Critical for explaining computational efficiency of ZKBIOWN.

title: poseidon-lite: Lightweight JavaScript implementation of Poseidon hash
notes: Poseidon8 implementation used in ZKBIOWN for field element hashing (BN254 field). Cite as software dependency.

title: On the Size of Pairing-Based Non-Interactive Arguments
notes: Groth16 SNARK system - the most widely used ZK proof system. Cite for explaining ZK proof generation/verification in ZKBIOWN.

title: Bulletproofs: Short Proofs for Confidential Transactions and More
venue: 2018 IEEE Symposium on Security and Privacy (SP)
notes: Bulletproofs - no trusted setup alternative to Groth16. Good for comparison in related work section.

title: PLONK: Permutations over Lagrange-bases for Oecumenical Noninteractive arguments of Knowledge
notes: PLONK - universal setup SNARK system. Alternative to Groth16 with different tradeoffs. Good for related work comparison.

title: Scalable Zero Knowledge with No Trusted Setup
notes: STARKs - post-quantum secure, no trusted setup. Good for discussing future-proofing in discussion section.

title: Noir: A Domain Specific Language for SNARK Proving Systems
notes: Noir DSL - domain-specific language for writing ZK circuits. Cite if discussing implementation tools.

title: A Data-Driven Approach to Cleaning Large Face Datasets
venue: 2014 IEEE International Conference on Image Processing (ICIP)
notes: FaceScrub dataset - 530 persons, 100k+ faces. ZKBIOWN uses filtered subset: 437 persons (≥2 captures per person) for intra-person validation. Baseline similarity: 63.94% same-person cosine similarity establishes dataset quality ceiling.

title: FaceNet: A Unified Embedding for Face Recognition and Clustering
venue: 2015 IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
notes: FaceNet - 128D and 512D embeddings used in ZKBIOWN experiments. Triplet loss training for face recognition. Implementation: https://github.com/davidsandberg/facenet

title: ArcFace: Additive Angular Margin Loss for Deep Face Recognition
venue: 2019 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
notes: ArcFace - 512D embeddings. State-of-the-art face recognition using additive angular margin loss. Implementation: https://github.com/deepinsight/insightface

title: face-api.js: JavaScript API for Face Recognition in the Browser with tensorflow.js
notes: face-api.js - 128D embeddings. Browser-based face recognition library used in ZKBIOWN experiments.

title: Zcash Protocol Specification, Version 2020.1.14 [Overwinter+Sapling+Blossom+Heartwood+Canopy]
notes: Pedersen Hash - traditional ZK-friendly hash (1.68 constraints/bit). Cite for performance comparison with Poseidon (0.2-0.5 constraints/bit).

title: Design of Symmetric-Key Primitives for Advanced Cryptographic Protocols
notes: Rescue Hash - alternative ZK-friendly hash using both x^5 and x^(1/5) S-boxes. Slower than Poseidon. Optional comparison baseline.

title: Fuzzy Extractors: How to Generate Strong Keys from Biometrics and Other Noisy Data
notes: Fuzzy Extractors - alternative biometric template protection. ONLY cite if explaining why NOT used (ZKBIOWN uses BioHashing instead).
